reasoning_model_1: Model 1 delivers the text accurately with no pronunciation errors. However, regarding the core instruction 'fast pace', the model performs poorly. The total duration is approximately 12 seconds for a relatively short sentence. There are distinct, lingering pauses at 00:02 (after 'report'), 00:04 (after 'review'), and 00:08 (after 'time'). These pauses break the momentum completely and result in a speaking rate that is moderate to slow, rather than fast. The tone is also somewhat robotic and lacks the urgency implied by a fast pace.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 delivers the text accurately and follows the 'fast pace' instruction very effectively. The total duration is significantly shorter (around 9 seconds) compared to Model 1. model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. The user explicitly requested a 'fast pace'. Model 1 failed to adhere to this, speaking at a normal-to-slow speed with frequent, flow-breaking pauses that extended the audio to 12 seconds. Model 2 successfully executed a rapid, urgent delivery, compressing the same text into about 9 seconds while maintaining high fluency and naturalness. Model 2 followed the specific paralinguistic instruction, whereas Model 1 did not.
